page: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 1 physical core and 1 vCPU running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 4 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for advanced vector extensions such as AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark results demonstrate a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for both stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. However, because the instance is limited to a single vCPU and physical core, multi-core benchmarks like Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core fall into the bottom 10% and bottom 25% respectively. Memory bandwidth performance is also constrained, with read-only small block bandwidth scoring in the bottom 10% and large block read-only bandwidth in the bottom 25%, though large block write bandwidth achieves an average rating.

In terms of specific workloads, the c7a.medium shows average performance in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and text processing. It performs poorly in database operations, software compilation, and static web serving, with Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput both placing in the bottom 10%. For lightweight machine learning, the instance achieves average text generation speeds of 107.26 tokens per second on a small 135M model, though prompt processing remains weak. Given its dedicated single-core compute profile and DDR5 memory, this instance offers an entry-level compute-optimized option for single-threaded tasks, lightweight utility scripts, and basic background processing where multi-threaded scaling is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized instance featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 1 vCPU, 1 physical core, and a 3.7 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and running on the Nitro hypervisor, it is equipped with 2.0 GB of DDR5 RAM at 5 GHz, with no local storage or baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench. However, due to its single-core limitation, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak to poor. The instance is suited for single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and lightweight utility workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized instance featuring 1 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPU, 2.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance, suited for single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A dedicated single-core AMD EPYC instance with DDR5 memory, delivering strong single-threaded performance for lightweight compute workloads.

2.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-core performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No bundled local storage and 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for single-threaded processing, lightweight utility scripts, and basic text processing
